title: mongoose.connect with tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true — the database TLS certificate is not validated (MITM)

params.passDetail: mongoose.connect validates the database server certificate (tlsAllowInvalidCertificates is not true) — the connection's TLS identity is verified.
params.failDetail: mongoose.connect is called with tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true, which turns OFF validation of the MongoDB server's TLS certificate. Anyone on the network path can present a forged certificate and silently man-in-the-middle the database connection — every query and credential is exposed. Remove tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true; supply the proper CA via `tlsCAFile` if a private CA is used.
params.absentCallDetail: No mongoose.connect call in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: mongoose.connect does not set tlsAllowInvalidCertificates; the secure default (validate) applies.

provenance.note: tlsAllowInvalidCertificates: true is the Mongo/mongoose equivalent of rejectUnauthorized: false — pasted to silence a self-signed-cert error against a managed cluster, it ships to production and disables MITM protection on the database connection.
